comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review advisory triage (PASS + signed). The advisories correctly flag that the ticket's `LLMConfig.from_env()` call-site index is STALE + INCOMPLETE — implementation must NOT trust the listed line numbers:

- STALE LINES: workflow_ops.py:329 is now a COMMENT (my #77 / drag-gripe-brake merge shifted lines); :113 likewise moved. Implementation will `grep -n "LLMConfig.from_env(" src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ src/rebar/llm/workflow/` and fix EVERY site, not the listed numbers (A1/E4/G1G2).
- MISSED SITE (critical T8/G6/A1): `plan_review_verify_inputs` (workflow_ops.py ~238) calls `_verifier_cfg(LLMConfig.from_env(...)).model` to size the verify request — must receive the resolved cfg/model identity too.
- gate_ops.completion_reconcile (~104) calls from_env ONLY for `cfg.repo_path` (not model/runner) — thread repo_path (or the resolved cfg identity) through inputs; document that this site needs repo_path, not just model/runner (G1G2).
- AC1 TEST (E5/E6/F1): add a DETERMINISTIC test that a caller passing an explicit `config=` (non-default model/runner) sees it in BOTH the LLM calls AND the verdict's model/runner fields — currently AC4 only says "existing tests pass". Will add to tests/unit/workflow/test_plan_review_workflow.py.
- CONTRACT (T5e): the new "ops read model/runner identity from ctx.inputs (threaded once at the run boundary) instead of re-deriving from env" is a workflow contract — document it at the produce_* boundary + the ops, mirroring the ticket_id threading pattern. Keep StepContext config-agnostic (per ticket scope).

Net: scope is broader than the ticket's line index implied (more from_env sites + the verify_inputs sizing path). No blocking issues. Will claim + implement after c38b (tarry-creep-range / PR #79) closes.

compiled_state.description:
Workflow gate ops re-resolve `LLMConfig.from_env()` independently instead of receiving the caller's resolved config, producing a DOCUMENTED correctness divergence: a library caller passing an explicit non-default `config=` has it honored for the LLM calls but the workflow verdict's `model`/`runner` fields still reflect the env config (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:78-79`).

## Why
Config resolution is a cross-cutting concern that should resolve once per run, not once per op. The current scatter silently ignores part of a caller's explicit configuration — a reliability/correctness gap for library/MCP consumers.

## What / files
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:113,329`, `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py:60,104`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:89` — stop calling `LLMConfig.from_env(...)` per op.
- Resolve `cfg` once at the run boundary (`gate_dispatch.produce_*` / `review_plan`) and pass the `runner`/`model` identity into the ops as workflow `inputs` (the pattern already used for `ticket_id`), so ops read it from `ctx.inputs` rather than re-deriving from env. Keep `StepContext` config-agnostic (`executor.py:107-120`).

## Scope
In: single-resolution + thread model/runner identity through inputs; remove the documented divergence note. Out: redesigning `StepContext` to carry the full `LLMConfig` type.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A caller passing an explicit `config=` sees that model/runner reflected in BOTH the LLM calls and the verdict's `model`/`runner` fields.
- [ ] No op calls `LLMConfig.from_env()` after the run boundary resolves it.
- [ ] The divergence caveat at `plan_review/__init__.py:78-79` is removed (no longer true).
- [ ] Existing plan-review/completion gate tests still pass.

file_impact.reason: resolve cfg once at the run boundary (produce_*) and thread model/runner identity into workflow inputs
stop per-op LLMConfig.from_env(); read model/runner from ctx.inputs
stop per-op LLMConfig.from_env(); read resolved identity from inputs
remove the documented model/runner divergence caveat
test: explicit config= reflected in verdict model/runner

compiled_state.title: Honor caller's explicit LLMConfig in workflow gate ops
